title: Lebanon: On the brink of war or peace? – European Council on Foreign Relations
gist: An ECFR podcast episode frames Lebanon as caught in the wider US-Israel-Iran war, with roughly 4,000 killed and over a million displaced since Hezbollah resumed attacks on Israel in March 2026, and examines whether the June trilateral framework deal between Israel, the US and Lebanon can hold as the broader US-Iran ceasefire unravels.

themes.theme: Lebanon's war toll and the trilateral deal

insights.claim: Hezbollah resumed direct attacks against Israel in March 2026 following the death of Iran's Supreme Leader, plunging Lebanon back into conflict; since then around 4,000 people in Lebanon have been killed and over a million displaced, with Israeli ground forces occupying parts of south Lebanon.
insights.speaker: Ellie Geranmayeh, Alia Moubayed, Randa Slim, Maha Yahya
insights.so_what: Concrete casualty and displacement figures (4,000 killed, 1 million+ displaced) quantify the severity of Lebanon's exposure to the wider Iran war, framing it among the most acutely affected fronts.
insights.locator: Lebanon is among the countries most impacted

insights.claim: In June 2026, Israel, the US and Lebanon signed a historic and controversial trilateral framework deal, with the parties meeting again in July to discuss implementation, including withdrawal of Israeli forces from occupied parts of Lebanon.
insights.speaker: Ellie Geranmayeh, Alia Moubayed, Randa Slim, Maha Yahya
insights.so_what: The trilateral deal's implementation phase, especially Israeli force withdrawal from south Lebanon, is a concrete near-term milestone to track for Lebanon's stability.
insights.locator: In June, Israel, the US and Lebanon signed a historic

insights.claim: The episode frames the durability of the Israel-US-Lebanon trilateral deal as unclear because the broader ceasefire between the US and Iran is unravelling, directly linking Lebanon's stability to the wider regional US-Iran conflict trajectory.
insights.speaker: Ellie Geranmayeh, Alia Moubayed, Randa Slim, Maha Yahya
insights.so_what: Lebanon's fragile peace is contingent on the separate US-Iran ceasefire holding — a renewed US-Iran escalation is a direct downside risk to the Lebanon deal.
insights.locator: But with a ceasefire between the US and Iran unravelling

source_meta.author: Ellie Geranmayeh, Alia Moubayed, Randa Slim, Maha Yahya
